acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_F001] PASS only if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:111 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:163 include at least one explicit hostile statement/action (not summarized as "threats" or "race war"), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:241 shows a direct consequence (fear behavior, withdrawal, or confrontation) tied to that hostility. FAIL if conflict remains abstracted.

[chapter_01_F002] PASS only if revised text anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:241 contains one fully dramatized exchange (question, refusal/pushback, response) and ends by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:245 with one immediate operational consequence visible on-page. FAIL if those spans remain mostly recap.

[chapter_01_F007] PASS only if the revised ending between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:315 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:339 includes one explicit enacted consequence (actor + mechanism + immediate effect on Lena's work), and Lena's final written objective clearly responds to that new constraint. FAIL if ending remains determination-only.

[F003] Pass if revised chapter 1 no longer states an unqualified throat-cut fatal mechanism that excludes the chapter 17 account; fail if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7 still definitively fixes cause as a throat cut with no compatibility room.

[F006] Pass if the revised block reads as three distinct voices under stress (not interchangeable policy cadence) and the token cluster no longer repeats as mantra; fail if three or more consecutive turns still read as similarly formal institutional prose.

[F010] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:137 no longer uses a still/flat facial formula and the replacement conveys a specific behavioral cue; fail if the revised line still relies on "went flat/went still" wording.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:111 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:163 include at least one explicit hostile statement/action (not summarized as "threats" or "race war"), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:241 shows a direct consequence (fear behavior, withdrawal, or confrontation) tied to that hostility. FAIL if conflict remains abstracted.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at key racial-pressure beats: the chapter names threat dynamics ("jokes and threats," "race war") but keeps the actual hostility abstract, which mutes the social violence driving character choices.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise target spans at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:113, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:164, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:243 to render one or two concrete threat utterances/actions (spoken, posted, or shouted) plus immediate material consequence for a named person on scene; avoid euphemistic summary labels.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if revised text anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:241 contains one fully dramatized exchange (question, refusal/pushback, response) and ends by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:245 with one immediate operational consequence visible on-page. FAIL if those spans remain mostly recap.
findings.problem: Timidity in escalation handling: critical midday pressure is compressed into procedural inventory language, so emotional and tactical consequence arrives as recap instead of lived scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the compressed transitions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:171, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:245 into in-room beats where resistance, fear, and command pressure collide in real time before the time-jump summary.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if the revised ending between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:315 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:339 includes one explicit enacted consequence (actor + mechanism + immediate effect on Lena's work), and Lena's final written objective clearly responds to that new constraint. FAIL if ending remains determination-only.
findings.problem: Consequence underpriced at chapter close: after a major chain-of-command rupture, the ending lands resolve language but not a concrete enacted penalty that reshapes immediate story options.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the closing section between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:315 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:339, add one specific administrative or operational repercussion from Clay's warning (documented restriction, access control, or resource constraint) that directly informs Lena's next-step note.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised chapter 1 no longer states an unqualified throat-cut fatal mechanism that excludes the chapter 17 account; fail if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7 still definitively fixes cause as a throat cut with no compatibility room.
findings.problem: Victim-injury logic conflicts with later confession: chapter 1 presents Voss with a deep throat cut exposing cartilage, while chapter 17's confession centers a trocar thrust below the sternum as the killing mechanism with no throat-cut accounting.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the wound description and early scene interpretation in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:163 so chapter 1 does not lock an incompatible mechanism. Keep observed brutality, but phrase causal certainty as provisional pending autopsy or include wound hierarchy that can coexist with a trocar-driven kill sequence.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised block reads as three distinct voices under stress (not interchangeable policy cadence) and the token cluster no longer repeats as mantra; fail if three or more consecutive turns still read as similarly formal institutional prose.
findings.problem: Dialogue register flattening and mantra diction. In this chapter, high-pressure exchange leans into repeated institutional token clusters (stability/order/posture/process), and Lena/Clay/Cora drift toward the same formal cadence instead of sharply separated idiolects.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the briefing exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:265, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:281, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:293, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:303. Keep Clay's polished control language, but give Lena and Cora more register divergence under pressure (more contractions, interruptions, and speaker-specific syntax rather than policy memo clauses).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:137 no longer uses a still/flat facial formula and the replacement conveys a specific behavioral cue; fail if the revised line still relies on "went flat/went still" wording.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book prose tic: default facial stillness/flatness appears across 4 chapters (1, 3, 10, 17). In this chapter, "eyes went flat" is one of the repeated constructions and should be replaced here.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the facial-default phrasing at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:137 with a character-specific reaction beat tied to Clay's controlling behavior (voice shift, gesture, physical displacement, or tactical word choice). Avoid the still/flat template in this slot.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
